preamble: Diphthongization of the nasalized low vowel ã into the rising diphthong ie, often occurring in stages of Romance development or dialectal nasal vowel breaking.

examples.note: Historical stages where nasalization triggered or accompanied vowel breaking.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
